tiles.name: Nanluoguxiang
tiles.funFact: This 800-year-old hutong alley is one of Beijing's best-preserved historical streets!

tiles.name: Houhai
tiles.funFact: Historic lake area where emperors once went ice skating!

tiles.name: Line 1
tiles.funFact: Beijing's first subway line, opened in 1971, running through Tiananmen Square!

tiles.name: Wangfujing
tiles.funFact: Beijing's premier shopping street, over 700 years old!

tiles.name: Sanlitun
tiles.funFact: Beijing's most international neighborhood, home to embassies and nightlife!

tiles.name: CBD
tiles.funFact: The Central Business District features the iconic CCTV Tower by Rem Koolhaas!

tiles.name: Dongcheng
tiles.funFact: The eastern district preserves countless historical hutongs and courtyard homes!

tiles.name: State Grid
tiles.funFact: The world's largest utility company, powering over 1.1 billion people!

tiles.name: Beihai Park
tiles.funFact: Imperial garden dating back to the 10th century, featuring the stunning White Dagoba!

tiles.name: Jingshan
tiles.funFact: This artificial hill offers the best panoramic view of the Forbidden City!

tiles.name: Line 2
tiles.funFact: Forms a rectangle around central Beijing, following the old city wall route!

tiles.name: Lama Temple
tiles.funFact: Home to a 26-meter tall Buddha carved from a single sandalwood tree!

tiles.name: Temple of Heaven
tiles.funFact: Where emperors prayed for good harvests! The Echo Wall can carry whispers 65 meters!

tiles.name: Summer Palace
tiles.funFact: UNESCO site covering 290 hectares, 75% water! Empress Cixi's marble boat still stands!

tiles.name: Drum Tower
tiles.funFact: Built in 1272, drummers once marked time for the entire city!

tiles.name: Bell Tower
tiles.funFact: Houses a massive 63-ton bronze bell from the Ming Dynasty!

tiles.name: Tiananmen
tiles.funFact: The largest public square in the world, holding up to 1 million people!

tiles.name: Line 4
tiles.funFact: Connects the Summer Palace to the south, carrying 1 million riders daily!

tiles.name: Chaoyang
tiles.funFact: Beijing's most international district, hosting the Olympic venues!

tiles.name: Olympic Park
tiles.funFact: Home to the Bird's Nest stadium and Water Cube from the 2008 Olympics!

tiles.name: Beijing Water
tiles.funFact: The South-North Water Transfer Project brings water from 1,400km away!

tiles.name: Zhongguancun
tiles.funFact: China's Silicon Valley, home to tech giants like Baidu and Lenovo!

tiles.name: Silk Street
tiles.funFact: Famous shopping market following the ancient Silk Road trading tradition!

tiles.name: Panjiayuan
tiles.funFact: Asia's largest antique market with over 3,000 vendors!

tiles.name: National Museum
tiles.funFact: The world's most visited museum with 7.4 million visitors annually!

tiles.name: Line 10
tiles.funFact: Forms a giant loop connecting all major districts of Beijing!

tiles.name: CCTV Tower
tiles.funFact: Standing 405 meters tall with a revolutionary "loop" design!

tiles.name: Forbidden City
tiles.funFact: The world's largest palace complex with 9,999 rooms, home to 24 emperors!
